ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a Subway chicken teriyaki meal set. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) is used as the closest parent. The cookie component is not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKK (chicken meat, cooked) is used as the closest defensible anchor for the primary protein component. No composite fast-food meal code exists in FoodEx2 for this product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept found for a composite fast-food chicken teriyaki sandwich meal with cookie.

main_ingredients: Subway cookie (chocolate chip or oatmeal raisin)

serving.description: 1 meal set (6-inch chicken teriyaki sub + 1 Subway cookie)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard meal set consisting of a 6-inch Subway Chicken Teriyaki sandwich (approximately 280 g) plus one Subway cookie (approximately 70 g), totalling approximately 350 g per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Chicken Teriyaki 6-inch sub and Subway Chocolate Chip Cookie — USDA Branded Food / Subway published nutrition data cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods) and Subway Nutrition Information
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry covers this exact composite meal (6-inch chicken teriyaki sub + cookie). Values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA Branded Food entry for Subway Chicken Teriyaki 6-inch sub (FDC ID 2103965, approximately 280 g) and (2) USDA Branded Food entry for Subway Chocolate Chip Cookie (FDC ID 2103980, approximately 70 g). Per-100g values are derived from the weighted composite of both components at a 280:70 ratio. This is an approved secondary source (USDA Branded Food) used for a commercial product. All values are expressed per 100 g of the combined meal.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of bread and sandwich products — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Subway Chicken Teriyaki meal with cookie. GI is estimated as a weighted composite: (1) Subway-style wheat sandwich bread GI ≈ 53 (University of Sydney GI Database; white wheat bread with mixed fillings typically 50–55); (2) Subway chocolate chip cookie GI ≈ 57 (analogous to oatmeal-raisin cookie, University of Sydney GI Database entry). Weighted composite GI for the 280 g sub + 70 g cookie meal = (53 × 280 + 57 × 70) / 350 ≈ 54, rounded to 53 for the sub-dominant component. gi_category set to 'low' (GI < 55). Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (53 × 130.2) / 100 ≈ 69.0 g (carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 37.2 × 3.5 = 130.2 g).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~1680 mg per meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal, as a single serving provides approximately 73% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg, primarily from the teriyaki sauce, bread, and processed cookie components.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High total carbohydrate and sugar load per serving (~130 g carbohydrate, ~40 g sugar per meal)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this meal with caution; the large carbohydrate load from the bread roll, teriyaki sauce (added sugars), and cookie can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation, and portion reduction or substitution with a lower-carbohydrate bread option is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium, saturated fat, and trans fat content from processed components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the high sodium (~1680 mg per serving), saturated fat (~9.1 g per serving), and presence of trans fat (~0.35 g per serving) from the cookie and teriyaki sauce warrant limiting consumption frequency and considering lower-sodium, lower-fat alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chicken Teriyaki Cookie Meal is a fast-food set meal sold at Subway restaurants in Singapore, consisting of a Subway Chicken Teriyaki submarine sandwich (available in 6-inch format) paired with one Subway cookie (typically chocolate chip or oatmeal raisin). The chicken teriyaki filling features grilled, marinated chicken breast coated in a sweet-savoury Japanese-style teriyaki sauce, assembled on a freshly baked wheat bread roll with standard Subway vegetable toppings including lettuce, tomato, onion, and green pepper. The cookie adds a sweet, buttery baked component to the meal. The flavour profile is a fusion of Japanese teriyaki sweetness with Western fast-food sandwich construction. Nutritionally, this is a high-carbohydrate, moderate-protein meal with significant sodium content due to the teriyaki sauce and bread. The meal provides approximately 938 kcal per serving (350 g), with around 130 g of carbohydrates, 47 g of protein, and 27 g of fat. The high sodium content (~1680 mg per serving) is a primary clinical concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The substantial carbohydrate and sugar load from the bread, teriyaki sauce, and cookie makes this meal a moderate-to-high concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The meal is halal-certified at Subway Singapore outlets. It is a popular lunch and dinner option among office workers and students in Singapore.

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA) contains a direct entry for this composite fast-food meal. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA Branded Food entries for the individual Subway components (sub and cookie) combined at a 280:70 g ratio. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using comparable bread and cookie entries. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. This record covers the Subway Chicken Teriyaki 6-inch sub + one Subway cookie meal set as sold at Subway Singapore (field survey listing). The 'Funan - Mix & Match' field dataing may represent a slightly different portion configuration; values are based on the standard 6-inch sub + cookie format. 2. Monounsaturated fat (monounsaturated_fat_g) is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA Branded Food entries used for this composite estimation. 3. Polyunsaturated fat (polyunsaturated_fat_g) is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA Branded Food entries used for this composite estimation. 4. Iron (iron_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source entries. 7. Vitamin C (vitamin_c_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source entries. 8. Vitamin A (vitamin_a_mcg_rae) is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source entries. 9. Vitamins B1, B2, B3, B6, B12 are -1 as they are not reported in the composite source entries. 10. Folate (folate_mcg_dfe) is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source entries. 11. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source entries. 12. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source entries. 13. Phosphorus (phosphorus_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source entries. 14. Magnesium (magnesium_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source entries. 15. Zinc (zinc_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source entries. 16. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source entries. 17. Water (water_g) is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source entries. 18. All -1 values reflect genuine absence of data in the approved source entries used; they are not confirmed zeros. 19. The food_name has been normalised from the field survey listing 'Chicken Teriyaki Cookie Meal' to 'Chicken Teriyaki Subway Meal with Cookie' for canonical clarity. The original field survey name is preserved in aliases. 20. Halal status is based on Subway Singapore's published halal certification; this should be verified periodically as certification status may change.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed. Identified that the food is a Singapore-served composite fast-food meal requiring multilingual naming for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi (Singapore context) plus Japanese (teriyaki origin). USDA Branded Food selected as approved source for commercial product. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries.

Iteration 2: Validator feedback indicated Q8 failure: 'aliases_by_language entries missing from flat aliases'. Reviewed aliases_by_language and flat aliases. Identified two missing entries in the flat aliases list: '照燒雞肉套餐（含曲奇）' (traditional Chinese, which had a simplified character '鸡' in the flat list) and 'Zhào shāo jī ròu tào cān (hán qū qí)' (romanized). Added these two exact strings to the flat 'aliases' array to ensure perfect consistency between aliases_by_language and aliases. Verified all -1 sentinel values are documented in notes. Verified GI is numeric > 0 (53) and gi_category is 'low'. Verified ontology match_type values are within allowed set (exact, closest_parent, none). Verified zero-macro justification not needed as no core macro is zero. Final self-score: 8/8, all hard gates pass. Decision: PASS.
